channel.title: 📢 Announcements - Frontity Community Forum
channel.description: Topics in the '📢 Announcements' category General news and announcements.

item.title: Frontity Framework is looking for maintainers
item.dc:creator: mmczaplinski
item.description:
The Frontity Framework is not under active development anymore. We have shared more details about this decision in this blog post.

This means that pull requests and issues are not being actively reviewed and bugs are not guaranteed to be fixed. There has been some interest from the community in continuing the project so if you are interested in becoming a maintainer and continuing the development of the framework please do let us know in this thread!

Thanks!

16 posts - 11 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Frontity Framework is looking for maintainers

item.title: Frontity is Joining Automattic
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
Hi folks, we wanted to share with you our latest news (just in case you missed it!): Frontity is joining Automattic, the parent company of WordPress.com, WooCommerce, Jetpack and Tumblr, among many other great products.

You can read the full announcement post here:

Frontity – 30 Aug 21

Frontity is joining Automattic

We're excited to announce that Frontity has been acquired by Automattic, the parent company of WordPress.com, WooCommerce, Tumblr and more.

We also prepared this Q&A page to address some of the questions you might have:

Frontity

Frontity Acquired by Automattic Q&A - Frontity.org

Frontity is joining Automattic. We understand that you may have questions regarding the acquisition. Here are a few answers.

Thank you for your continued support, and for being a part of this great community! We’ll keep you posted about any important updates.

6 posts - 4 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Frontity is Joining Automattic

item.title: Announcing a New Version of the Frontity Docs
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
We’re delighted to announce that we’ve released a new structure of our Docs to make them more comprehensible

Documentation is one of our key priorities here at Frontity and we want it to be the best possible resource for our community.

After several months of work, we’re excited to announce that we have revamped our documentation to bring you more helpful resources and improve your experience with Frontity.

In addition to our recently announced step-by-step tutorial, we’ve published a new API reference site and reorganised our docs across three different sites:

  • Step-by-step tutorial
  • Conceptual understanding / Guides
  • API Reference

The goal of the changes we’ve made is to provide you with the most useful information in the most appropriate context as and when you need it.

For more information see our blog post announcing its publication.

Frontity – 18 Feb 21

Announcing a New Version of the Frontity Docs

We're excited to announce that we have revamped our docs to bring you more helpful resources and improve your experience with Frontity.

3 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Announcing a New Version of the Frontity Docs

item.title: Announcing our Partnership with 10up
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We are excited to announce our partnership with 10up, one of the largest WordPress-centric agencies in the world, to keep pushing our mission of helping people build websites with WordPress and React easily.

We’re very happy to join forces with such a great team, and believe that their expertise and contributions will have a very positive impact on the project.

Check out our blog post to learn more about the reasons behind this partnership and discover 10up’s latest contributions to the framework:

Frontity – 23 Oct 20

Announcing Our Partnership with 10up

We're thrilled to announce our partnership with 10up to keep pushing our mission of helping people build websites with WordPress and React easily.

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Announcing our Partnership with 10up

item.title: Documentation Updates Q3-2020
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
This thread shows relevant Documentation Updates during Q3-2020

3 posts - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Documentation Updates Q3-2020

item.title: Docs update: new Troubleshooting page and other changes
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We made some changes to our docs recently:

  • CLI Commands: we reorganized this section to group commands and add information about Environment Variables.

  • Troubleshooting: we created this new page to explain common issues detected in Frontity projects and how to solve them.

  • Guides: we moved some pages, such as React, JavaScript and SEO, under this section and improved its description.

In addition, we renamed some other sections:

  • Learning Frontity can be found now as Core Concepts.
  • Frontity Packages as Packages.
  • Frontity CLI as CLI Commands.

We’ll keep improving and iterating on the documentation. Feel free to ask if you have any questions.

Community contributions to the documentation are always welcome. You can read more on how to contribute here.

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Docs update: new Troubleshooting page and other changes

item.title: Introducing the New Series of Videos "Frontity Talks"
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We are happy to share with you an initiative we’ve been working on recently: Frontity Talks, a new series of videos created by our DevRel team (@juanma and @mburridge) to explain different topics and concepts about Frontity.

Read more on these sessions and how you can suggest topics for the next videos in our blog post:

Frontity – 8 Jul 20

Introducing the New Series of Videos "Frontity Talks"

Introducing Frontity Talks, a series of videos created by our DevRel team with the goal of explaining different topics about Frontity.

As always, feel free to reach out with any questions you might have!

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Introducing the New Series of Videos "Frontity Talks"

item.title: Docs update: `wp-source` package improvements
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
We have reviewed and updated the wp-source package documentation page → https://docs.frontity.org/api-reference-1/wordpress-source

Now, there’s a more detailed explanation of the API in its methods and better examples of how to use these methods in a React component ( useEffect )

In that page, you can also find links to some videos where Frontity DevRel team talks about the wp-source package

  • Frontity DevRel Talks 2020-01 - wp-source & CSS In JS [1:36]
  • Frontity DevRel Talks 2020-02 - Pagination example & wp-source (state & fetch) [17:53]

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Docs update: `wp-source` package improvements

item.title: New Docs: Best Practices for Sharing your Frontity Project
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
New in the docs: Best practices for sharing your Frontity project.

This is the recommended way of creating and sharing your custom Frontity theme with the right structure, which is especially important when:

  • Sharing a project in the Showcases category.
  • When asking for support from the community regarding your project.

Feel free to ask if you have any questions.

Community contributions to the documentation are always welcome. You can read more on how to contribute here .

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: New Docs: Best Practices for Sharing your Frontity Project

item.title: New Docs: Frontity CLI + SEO + Showcase sections
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We’re happy to announce that we have recently published new Frontity documentation, featuring:

  • CLI Commands: how to use Frontity CLI and a list of the commands you have available.
  • SEO: how to customize meta tags and robots.txt in Frontity.
  • Showcases: a new section to share the awesome projects the community is building with Frontity. This will help us and others learn, get inspired and see what can be accomplished with the framework. We’d love to see your Frontity site in the list!

Update: the Showcase page has been moved to https://frontity.org/showcase.

We’ll keep improving and iterating on the documentation. Feel free to let us know if you have any feedback about the new pages.

Community contributions to the documentation are always welcome. You can read more on how to contribute here.

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: New Docs: Frontity CLI + SEO + Showcase sections

item.title: [General Announcement] New Frontity.org website
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We are happy to share with you that we recently launched a brand new frontity.org website!

  • It’s built with WordPress, Gutenberg and Frontity, and aims to improve the way the framework is explained, its features and how it works.
  • It also features a new About Us page so you can learn more about our story, the framework’s mission and our philosophy.

Here’s the GitHub repo of the project. If you find any error, please feel free to open an issue.

GitHub

frontity/frontity.org

The Frontity project of the frontity.org site. Contribute to frontity/frontity.org development by creating an account on GitHub.

We are also planning to write a case study on how we built the website and what we have learned while using Gutenberg and Frontity.

Special thanks to the designer Jose Cruz and to @SantosGuillamot, @iamuchejude, @luisherranz, @David, @mmczaplinski from our team for their amazing work!

We hope you like the new website as much as we do!

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: [General Announcement] New Frontity.org website

item.title: Docs Update: "Getting Started" Section Improvements + New "Quick Start Guide" Video
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We have recently made some changes to our docs:

  • Improved the Getting Started section, which now includes two new pages: Connect Frontity to Your WordPress Site and Next Steps.
  • Created a new explanatory video for the Quick Start Guide.
  • Added a new About Frontity section with two pages: Frontity Features and Get involved.
  • Moved the JS and React guides to the Key Concepts section.
  • The Welcome to Frontity page now links to the different sections of the docs.

Thanks @mburridge and @juanma!

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Docs Update: "Getting Started" Section Improvements + New "Quick Start Guide" Video

item.title: [General Announcement] Introducing our new Developer Relations team
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We are excited to introduce our new Developer Relations team formed by JuanMa Garrido (@juanma) and Michael Burridge (@mburridge).

JuanMa and Michael will be taking a meaningful role in shaping the experience devs have with Frontity, improving our documentation and creating learning resources for the community, among many other things.

Welcome, folks! We’re happy to have you on board!

5 posts - 5 participants

Read full topic
item.source: [General Announcement] Introducing our new Developer Relations team

item.title: Docs Update: How to Install a new Frontity package + Frontity Themes section
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
We have recently done some updates to our docs:

  • Added a guide on how to install a new Frontity package.
  • Added a new Frontity Themes section.
  • Improved the Code Contributions guide.

Thanks @SantosGuillamot and @luisherranz!

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Docs Update: How to Install a new Frontity package + Frontity Themes section

item.title: [General Announcement] Introducing our Technical SEO Expert Christian Oliveira
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
One of the main concerns when building a headless WordPress site with React is SEO and how to do it right.

From now on, Christian Oliveira (@christianoliveira) is joining the Frontity community and will be our Technical SEO Expert to help answer all your questions about technical SEO.

Welcome and thanks for joining us, Christian!

4 posts - 4 participants

Read full topic
item.source: [General Announcement] Introducing our Technical SEO Expert Christian Oliveira

item.title: [Upcoming] New Frontity Theme with Chakra UI
item.dc:creator: Segun
item.description:
I’m currently working on a new Frontity theme powered by the amazing Chakra UI library.

Here’s a link to the repo: https://github.com/chakra-ui/frontity-chakra-ui-theme

7 posts - 4 participants

Read full topic
item.source: [Upcoming] New Frontity Theme with Chakra UI

item.title: [Upcoming] REST API Head Tags plugin for WordPress
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
We are about to release the REST API Head Tags plugin. It adds a head_tags field for each entity, containing a list of head tags in JSON format, and it is intended to work with any SEO plugin that modifies the content of the <head> element.

The progress can be tracked here: https://github.com/frontity/wp-org-plugins/pull/10

Also, a new Frontity package named @frontity/head-tags will be developed to directly integrate with this plugin from any Frontity project.

6 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: [Upcoming] REST API Head Tags plugin for WordPress

item.title: [Upcoming] New Twenty Twenty Frontity theme
item.dc:creator: Segun
item.description:
The upcoming Twenty Twenty WordPress theme is currently been ported over to Frontity. It will available as a free Frontity theme.

You can track our progress here: https://github.com/frontity/frontity/pull/235

We hope to add support for CodeSandbox so you can try this awesome Frontity theme directly from your browser without having to install anything.

6 posts - 5 participants

Read full topic
item.source: [Upcoming] New Twenty Twenty Frontity theme

item.title: Docs update: 06 September 2019 - Learning Frontity guide
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
We’ve added more pages to Learning Frontity (and new info to the existing ones) to make it easier to master Frontity :

  • Project - We’ve added an introductory page with the basics of how Frontity works and a common project structure.
  • Styles - New page with the info necessary to style your theme with Frontity.
  • Code Splitting - Info about how to split your code with Frontity in order to improve the performance of your app.
  • Head - How to add tags like <title>, <meta>, or others to your <head> section.

We’ve also added new info to the existing pages such as Libraries or Namespaces. If you are interested, we recommend you to take a look at the whole Learning Frontity section.

We’re planning to release soon new pages into this section explaining how Router and Source work.

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Docs update: 06 September 2019 - Learning Frontity guide

item.title: Docs update: 11 July 2019 - JS and React basics
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
We keep improving our docummentation:

  • Add “Javascript basics” to explain which concepts are needed to understand Frontity (thanks to luisherranz & SantosGuillamot)
  • Add “React basics” to explain which concepts are needed to understand Frontity (thanks to luisherranz & SantosGuillamot)

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Docs update: 11 July 2019 - JS and React basics
